(Romanian pronunciation: [[mi.oˈvenʲ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Romanian "Help:IPA/Romanian")) is a town in [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County"), [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), approximately 15 km (9.3 mi) north-east of [Pitești](/wiki/Pite%C8%99ti "Pitești"). As of 2021[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mioveni&action=edit), it had a population of 29,317. The town administers four villages: Clucereasa, Colibași, Făgetu, and Racovița.
["2021 Romanian census"](https://www.recensamantromania.ro/rezultate-rpl-2021/rezultate-definitive-caracteristici-demografice/). National Institute of Statistics. 1 December 2021.
["Results of the 2024 local elections"](https://prezenta.roaep.ro/locale09062024v2/). Central Electoral Bureau. Retrieved 17 December 2025.

**Mioveni** (Romanian pronunciation: [[mi.oˈvenʲ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Romanian "Help:IPA/Romanian")) is a town in [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County"), [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), approximately 15 km (9.3 mi) north-east of [Pitești](/wiki/Pite%C8%99ti "Pitești"). As of 2021[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mioveni&action=edit), it had a population of 29,317. The town administers four villages: Clucereasa, Colibași, Făgetu, and Racovița.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mioveni&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Mioveni was first mentioned in a written record in 1485. It developed much in the 1970s after the construction of the [Automobile Dacia](/wiki/Automobile_Dacia "Automobile Dacia") manufacturing plant, inaugurated in 1968. There is also a Nuclear Research Institute, that builds components and materials for the [Cernavodă Nuclear Power Plant](/wiki/Cernavod%C4%83_Nuclear_Power_Plant "Cernavodă Nuclear Power Plant"), and a high security prison.

It officially became a town in 1989, as a result of the [Romanian rural systematization program](/wiki/Romanian_rural_systematization_program "Romanian rural systematization program"). Prior to April 1989, when it was declared a town, the place was a [commune](/wiki/Communes_of_Romania "Communes of Romania") under the name of *Colibași*. In 1996, the historic name of Mioveni was revived, although the old village had been completely razed under the [Communist regime](/wiki/Communist_Romania "Communist Romania") in order to make way for new urban construction.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mioveni&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Economy")]

[Automobile Dacia](/wiki/Automobile_Dacia "Automobile Dacia") is headquartered in the town. The company's single plant here has a production capacity of 350,000 vehicles per year.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mioveni&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Sports")]

Its [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") team, [CS Mioveni](/wiki/CS_Mioveni "CS Mioveni") (formerly *Dacia Mioveni*) has played four seasons in the [Romanian first league](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"): in [2007–08](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Liga_I "2007–08 Liga I"), [2011–12](/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_Liga_I "2011–12 Liga I"), [2021–22](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_Liga_I "2021–22 Liga I") and [2022–23](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_Liga_I "2022–23 Liga I"). They also reached the semifinals of the [2007–08 Romanian Cup](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "2007–08 Cupa României").

[Stadionul Orășenesc](/wiki/Stadionul_Or%C4%83%C8%99enesc_(Mioveni) "Stadionul Orășenesc (Mioveni)") (which opened in 2000) is the home ground for the Mioveni team.

## Natives

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mioveni&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Natives")]

* [Alexandru Ișfan](/wiki/Alexandru_I%C8%99fan "Alexandru Ișfan") (born 2000), footballer
* [Andrei Panait](/wiki/Andrei_Panait "Andrei Panait") (born 1989), footballer

## Twin cities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mioveni&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Twin cities")]

* [![Hungary](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c1/Flag_of_Hungary.svg/40px-Flag_of_Hungary.svg.png)](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") [Kiskunlacháza](/wiki/Kiskunlach%C3%A1za "Kiskunlacháza") – [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary")
* [![Hungary](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c1/Flag_of_Hungary.svg/40px-Flag_of_Hungary.svg.png)](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") [Nagyvázsony](/wiki/Nagyv%C3%A1zsony "Nagyvázsony") – [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary")
* [![Bulgaria](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9a/Flag_of_Bulgaria.svg/40px-Flag_of_Bulgaria.svg.png)](/wiki/Bulgaria "Bulgaria") [Petrich](/wiki/Petrich "Petrich") – [Bulgaria](/wiki/Bulgaria "Bulgaria")
